summary.intent: Update the gstack section in CLAUDE.md to map each gstack skill to its corresponding Stokowski pipeline stage, as requested in a review comment on a rework run of CHA-6.
summary.outcome: CLAUDE.md was updated with a skills-to-Stokowski-stages mapping, committed, and pushed to the existing PR (https://github.com/samratjp/moltis/pull/2). A Linear comment was posted summarizing the completed work.

repo: The project uses a Stokowski orchestration workflow with 8 stages: investigate, research-review, implement, implementation-review, code-review, merge-review, merge, done — alternating between agent and human-gate stages.
gstack skills are vendored into the project at .claude/skills/gstack with .git removed so teammates can run ./setup without needing the original repo.
.gitignore excludes local skill symlinks created by ./setup since they are machine-specific.
CLAUDE.md documents the gstack skills table and teammate setup instructions.

code.finding: gstack section updated to map 13 skills (/browse, /plan-ceo-review, /plan-eng-review, /plan-design-review, /review, /ship, /qa, /qa-only, /qa-design-review, /setup-browser-cookies, /retro, /document-release, /gstack-upgrade) to their corresponding Stokowski lifecycle stages.

workflow: Stokowski uses a Linear comment titled '## Workpad' as a persistent workpad across runs — new content is appended, not overwritten.
The orchestration prompt distinguishes first-run vs rework-run behavior: on rework, find existing branch/PR, read review comments, address feedback, push new commits (no force-push).
Linear API key authentication was failing at one point, requiring investigation of what credentials Stokowski provides.
The mcp__plugin_context-mode tool (ctx_execute, ctx_search) is used extensively to read Stokowski stage definitions and project context.

friction: Linear API key authentication failed during the previous run, causing repeated retries across many mcp__plugin_context-mode__ctx_execute calls before a workaround was found.
The initial run required force-pushing an amended commit, which is generally discouraged and suggests the commit workflow needed correction mid-session.
open_items: The rework was triggered by a review comment asking to map skills to Stokowski stages — the PR (https://github.com/samratjp/moltis/pull/2) is now in implementation-review state and still needs human approval before proceeding to code-review.
Stokowski transitions the issue from 'complete' to 'implementation-review' after this run — downstream stages (code-review, merge-review, merge) are still pending.
